zh.Oviedo, Asturias, Spain: 西班牙阿斯圖里亞斯奧維多
zh.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña是Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara和Usenda Díaz de Velasco的兒子，於1120年出生在西班牙阿斯圖里亞斯奧維多。他與Elvira Pérez de Azagra結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Oviedo, Asturias, Spain: 西班牙阿斯圖里亞斯奧維多
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña是Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara和Usenda Díaz de Velasco的儿子，于1120年出生在西班牙阿斯圖里亞斯奧維多。他与Elvira Pérez de Azagra结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Rodríguez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Pedro, Raul, Miguel, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Ligia, Catarina, Paulo, Joaquim, Omero, Joao, Wenseslao, Adauto, Albeiro, Godofredo.

Spanish (Rodríguez) and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Rodrigo . Rodriguez (including in the sense 2 below) is the ninth most frequent surname in the US. Compare De Rodriguez and Rodriquez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Rodiguez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Adriana, Alvaro, Ana Luz, Angel, Armando, Arnulfo.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodriquez
meanings: Spanish (Rodríquez): variant of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

variants.name: Rodriges
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodreguez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: las
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name, nickname, or randomly assigned by Polish clerks, from Polish las ‘forest, wood’.
Jewish (American): shortened form of some original Ashkenazic surname.

nameMeanings.name: Asturias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from the Asturias region in northern Spain, named by the Romans for its contemporary inhabitants, the Astures.

nameMeanings.name: Nava
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Pedro, Manuel, Alfredo, Francisco, Ruben, Javier, Enrique.

Spanish, Catalan, and Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Nava, named with nava ‘treeless plateau’, a word of pre-Roman origin, most probably from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’ (see Navarra ).

variants.name: Navejas
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): probably a habitational name from a place in Spain so named, which no longer exists; it is a derivative of   Nava  . This surname is recorded in Spain in the 16th century, but has died out there.

variants.name: Navalta
meanings: Spanish: probably a topographic name from Sierra de la Nava Alta, a mountain range in Aragon (see   Nava  ). This surname no longer exists in Spain; it is common in the Philippines.

variants.name: Naba
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of   Nava  .
Japanese: rare name found mostly in central Japan, written phonetically 那波 ‘what’ and ‘waves’, 名場 ‘name’ and ‘place’, or 名葉 ‘name’ and ‘leaves’.

variants.name: Navarra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Edgardo, Guido, Santo, Vito, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonio, Benedicto, Camillo, Dante, Emilio, Fausto, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Mario, Romulo, Sergio.  Spanish Agustin, Herlinda, Manuel, Solita.

Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from Navarre (Castilian Navarra, Basque Nafarroa, French Navarre), a largely Basque-speaking region now divided between Spain and France, but in the Middle Ages an independent Basque kingdom. The name of the region derives from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’. Compare Navarre .

variants.name: Navas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Jose, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Roberto, Armando, Arturo, Alejandro, Carlos, Enrique, Francisco.
Asturian-Leonese and Spanish: plural form of   Nava  .
In some cases possibly Catalan (Navàs): habitational name from Navars in Barcelona province, Catalonia (Spain), denoting a place where people from Navarre lived.

variants.name: Navar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Joaquin, Jose, Raul, Alfredo, Arturo, Erasmo, Ezequiel, Gilberto, Juan, Manuel, Miguel.
Spanish: probably a topographic name from a collective form ofnava ‘treeless plateau’ (see Nava ).

nameMeanings.name: Noroña
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish (Noroña): altered form of Asturian-Leonese Noreña (see Norena ). The surname Noroña is virtually non-existing in Spain.

variants.name: Lorona
meanings: Hispanic (Loroña): altered form of Spanish Noroña (see Norona ).

variants.name: Noronha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Augusto, Jose, Adelio, Alda, Alfredo, Fernando, Frederico, Luis, Manuel, Mauricio, Santana.  Portuguese Guilherme, Joaquim.  Indian Anil, Arun, Priya, Roshni, Sunita.
Portuguese: from Spanish Noroña (see Norona ). This surname is common in the former Portuguese colony of Goa and elsewhere in western India, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists.

variants.name: Norena
meanings: Asturian-Leonese (Noreña): habitational name from a place called Noreña in Asturias. In Spain, this surname is most common in Salamanca and Cantabria; it is not found in Asturias.

nameMeanings.name: Alvaro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: of uncertain etymology.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña was born in 1120, in Oviedo, Asturias, Spain as the son of Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara and Usenda Díaz de Velasco. He married Elvira Pérez de Azagra. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña est né en 1120, à Oviedo, Asturias, Espagne, fils de Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara et Usenda Díaz de Velasco. Il a épousé Elvira Pérez de Azagra. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña wurde 1120 in Oviedo, Asturias, Spanien, als Sohn von Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara und Usenda Díaz de Velasco geboren. Er hat Elvira Pérez de Azagra geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña è nato il nel 1120 nel Oviedo, Asturias, Spagna come figlio di Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara e Usenda Díaz de Velasco. Lui sposato Elvira Pérez de Azagra. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.Oviedo, Asturias, Spain: スペインアストゥリアスオヴィエド
ja.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroñaは1120年にスペインアストゥリアスオヴィエドでRodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de LaraとUsenda Díaz de Velascoの息子として生まれた。Elvira Pérez de Azagraと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña(은)는 1120년 스페인 애스투리아스 비도에서 Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara(와)과 Usenda Díaz de Velasco의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Elvira Pérez de Azagra(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña nasceu em 1120, em Oviedo, Asturias, Espanha filho de Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara e Usenda Díaz de Velasco. Ele casou-se com Elvira Pérez de Azagra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña родился в 1120 г. в местности овиедо, астуряс, Испания. Его родителей звали Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara и Usenda Díaz de Velasco. Он женился на женщине по имени Elvira Pérez de Azagra. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Alvaro Rodríguez de las Asturias Nava y Noroña nació en 1120, en Oviedo, Asturias, España como hijo de Rodrigo Álvarez de las Asturias Perez de Lara y Usenda Díaz de Velasco. Se casó con Elvira Pérez de Azagra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
